title: Spice-Crusted Tuna & Red Pepper Beans
summary: Big on protein. This smoky bean stew with spice-crusted tuna is the answer to all your weeknight dinner dilemmas.

method.description: Finely dice the onion and thinly slice the garlic, zest the lemon. Drain the red peppers and finely chop them. Pick the parsley leaves and thinly slice the stems.
Cover the tuna steaks in the Cajun seasoning, pressing to coat all over. Season with salt and set aside.

Heat a saucepan over a medium heat with half the olive oil. Once hot, add the onion and cook, stirring, for 4–5 mins until soft and translucent. Add the garlic, parsley stems and tomato purée, then cook for 2–4 mins, stirring, until the purée darkens.

Tip in the white beans and their liquid, followed by the chopped red peppers. Season with salt and black pepper, then bring to the boil. Turn the heat down to low and let it simmer gently while you cook the tuna. Add a splash of water to loosen if needed.

Heat a frying pan over a medium-high heat with the remaining olive oil. Once hot, add the tuna steaks and sear for 3–5 mins on each side, or until nicely coloured and beginning to char. Remove from the heat and let them rest for a couple of minutes.

Taste the beans and adjust with more salt, pepper and lemon juice, if needed. Spoon the beans into bowls. Slice the tuna and lay it over the top. Finish with a sprinkle of lemon zest, parsley leaves and any remaining lemon wedges on the side.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
